summary: Backend testing of the 6-step audit (Admissions / Health / Discipline / Re-Enrollment + RBAC fixes on /schools and /report-templates). Created /app/backend/tests/test_audit_modules.py with 31 tests covering auth, full CRUD on each new module, role-gate checks (admin vs teacher vs unauthenticated), and cross-tenant isolation via SUNF and RVSD seeds. All 31 tests passed (100%).
passed_tests: POST/PUT/GET/DELETE /api/admissions full CRUD round-trip, school_code stamped to SUNF, 404 after delete
GET /api/health/{rvsd_student_id} (called by SUNF admin) -> 404 cross-tenant isolation
POST /api/health/{id}/vaccination, /allergy, /visit -> 200 with appended entry visible in response
DELETE /api/health/{id}/vaccination/{entry_id} -> 200 and entry removed on re-GET
Health route blocks unauthenticated -> 401/403 (parent creds not provided in test_credentials.md, see note)
POST /api/discipline with RVSD student via SUNF admin -> 404
POST /api/enrollment/execute with action=no_change -> 200 with matching counts (no DB mutation)
GET /api/schools as superuser -> all schools (SUNF & RVSD present)
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2961 lines — strongly recommend splitting modules (admissions/health/discipline/enrollment) into separate routers to stay under the 700-line guideline.

test_credentials.md does not list any PARENT account. The spec required asserting parent -> 403 on /api/health; we substituted unauthenticated -> 401/403 instead. Add a parent test user so future runs can assert the explicit 403 from require_roles.
/api/enrollment/execute returns BOTH withdrew and withdrawn keys (legacy alias). Consider deprecating one to reduce ambiguity.

_append_health_entry pushes a payload that already contains an `id` key but spreads `{'id': uuid, **entry}` — order is fine, however if a caller ever passes id in the payload it would be silently overridden. Tighten the Pydantic models if intentional.
test_credentials: Used SUNF admin/Admin@123, RVSD admin/Admin@123, SUNF teacher sarah.thompson.sunf/Teacher@123, JTECH superuser jtech.innovations@outlook.com/Xekleidoma@1 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). No parent account available.
seed_data_creation: Tests created/then-deleted ephemeral admission, vaccination, allergy, visit and discipline records in SUNF (all prefixed TEST_). Enrollment/execute test used action=no_change so no student row mutation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend audit endpoints are stable. test_audit_modules.py can be re-run as a regression suite (15s). When parent test creds are added, replace TestHealth.test_parent_403's unauthenticated check with an authenticated parent token to assert the explicit 403 from require_roles.
